summary: Backend modular refactoring verification COMPLETE. All 51 API tests passed. The modular code structure (server.py, db.py, auth.py, models.py, helpers.py, encryption.py, storage.py, and routes/) is fully functional after refactoring from the monolithic 1453-line server.py.
passed_tests: Auth: POST /api/auth/register - creates admin with own org_id
Auth: POST /api/auth/login - returns token and user data
Auth: GET /api/auth/me - returns current user info (without password_hash)
Org: GET /api/org/owner - returns organization owner details
Cars: GET /api/cars - list cars for org
Cars: PATCH /api/cars/{id} - sold_by auto-population when status=Satıldı
Cars: PATCH /api/cars/{id} - status revert clears sold_by fields
Stats: GET /api/stats - returns all dashboard statistics fields
Permissions: PUT /api/permissions - admin can update permissions
Users: POST /api/users - create user in org (admin only)
Users: DELETE /api/users/{id} - delete user (admin only, can't delete self)
Users: GET /api/employees - list employees for dropdown
Users: GET /api/org-users - list org users for filtering
Multi-tenant: Org2 cannot see Org1's cars
Multi-tenant: Org2 cannot modify Org1's car (404)
Multi-tenant: Org2 cannot delete Org1's car (404)
critical_code_review_comments: Modular architecture correctly implemented: server.py (113 lines) is now a slim entry point
All routes properly separated into /app/backend/routes/ modules
db.py correctly initializes MongoDB connection from environment variables
auth.py properly handles JWT token creation/validation and password hashing
helpers.py build_data_filter() correctly enforces org_id isolation
Multi-tenant isolation verified: users from different orgs cannot see/modify each other's data
Role-based access verified: non-admin users get 403 for admin-only operations
Soft delete pattern consistent across cars, customers, transactions, appointments
sold_by auto-population logic works correctly when status set to 'Satıldı'

test_credentials.note: Each test class creates unique test users with prefix TEST_MODULAR_

seed_data_creation: Test creates multiple test users and test data (cars, customers, transactions, appointments) prefixed with TEST_MODULAR_ for cleanup identification
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend modular refactoring verified - all 51 API endpoints work correctly. Test file /app/backend/tests/test_modular_backend.py covers all CRUD operations for all modules (auth, cars, customers, transactions, appointments, users, stats, permissions, exports, encryption). Multi-tenancy isolation and role-based access control also verified. No issues found.

mocked_apis.Email sending: MOCKED - verification code returned in register response
